title: TWO-OBJECT TWO-BIDDER SIMULTANEOUS AUCTIONS
abstract: Symmetric equilibria are constructed for a class of symmetric auction games. The games all have two identical bidders bidding in two simultaneous sealed-bid auctions for identical objects. Information is complete and the objects are either complements or substitutes. In both cases a continuum of mixed-strategy equilibria are identified. All these equilibria have a surprising structure: The supports of all the mixtures that generate equilibria are two one-dimensional curves, and they surround a two-dimensional set of pure best responses.
summary_en: When two bidders bid simultaneously in two separate sealed-bid auctions for identical objects, what equilibria can arise? Szentes constructs symmetric equilibria for this class of auction games. With complete information and either complementary or substitute objects, a continuum of mixed-strategy equilibria are identified. The structural finding is striking: the supports of all the mixtures generating equilibria are two one-dimensional curves, surrounded by a two-dimensional set of pure best responses. This curve-and-region structure is unusual in the auction-equilibrium literature and has implications for designing simultaneous-bidding mechanisms.
data_used: Theoretical paper. Game-theoretic analysis of simultaneous sealed-bid auctions with two bidders. Mathematical construction of mixed-strategy equilibria.
policy_relevance: Indirect relevance to the Hungarian Public Procurement Authority (Közbeszerzési Hatóság) and to MEKH spectrum / energy-block auctions: simultaneous-bid designs are common in practice but their multi-equilibrium structure is rarely modelled. The paper's curve-and-region equilibrium geometry suggests the welfare ranking of simultaneous vs sequential auctions may be more delicate than commonly assumed in policy reviews.
